rulings.Case Title: KYLE BACH VS. TIMOTHY BAKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Stay Arbitration And Award Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 14, 2007, LINE 3, DEFENDANT T. BAKER CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA, TIMOTHY BAKER'S Notioc Of Motion And Motion To Stay Arbitration And Award Sanctions. THE MOTION IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S AUTHORITY UNDER CCP 128(a)(8). PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO DISMISS THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS BY ENGAGING IN EXTENSIVE DISCOVERY IN THIS CASE BEFORE SEEKING TO COMMENCE THE ARBITRATION AGAINST ONLY ONE PARTY TO THIS DISPUTE. THE RECORD ESTABLISHES THE VERY REALISTIC POSSIBLILITY OF INCONSISTENT AND/OR CONFLICTING RULINGS IF THE STAY IS NOT ISSUED. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED, CCP 128.5 IS INAPPLICABLE BECAUSE THIS ACTION WAS COMMENCED AFTER 12/31/94. =(302/PJB/PB)

rulings.Case Title: TANYA MURILLO VS. JEANNINE E CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 14, 2007, LINE 4, PLAINTIFF TANYA MURILLO'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO ALLOW A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE TO REMOVE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING AND TO MAKE OTHER MINOR CHANGES AS REFLECTED IN THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT FILED ON JULY 20, 2007. THE REQUEST TO AMEND TO ADD A 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUANTUM MERRIT IS DENIED FOR IT WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. =(302/PJB/KD)

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP INC IN THE INTEREST OF THE VS. PRO GENERAL ROOFING INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judicial Approval Of Settlement Between Plaintiff Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. And Defendant Associated Building Enterprises, Inc. Declaration Of Reuben Yeroushalmi In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 14, 2007, LINE 5, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP INC IN THE INTEREST OF THE PUBLIC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judicial Approval Of Settlement Between Plaintiff Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. And Defendant Associated Building Enterprises, Inc. Declaration Of Reuben Yeroushalmi In Support Thereof. HEARING REQUIRED. PLAINTIF'S COUNSEL MUST SUPPLEMENT THE RECORD TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE FEES REQUESTED ARE CONSISTENT WITH MARKET RATES FOR LAWYERS WITH COMPARABLE EXPERIENCE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAVE NOT DEMONSTRATED HOW THE PAYMENT IN LIEU OF A CIVIL PENALTY WILL BE SPENT FOR A PROPER, DESIGNATED PURPOSE, HOW THE RECIPIENT WAS SELECTED, WHETHER IT IS APPROPRIATE FOR PLAINTIFF TO SELECT ITSELF AND WHAT PROCEDURES ARE IN PLACE TO INSURE ACCOUNTABILITY. =(302/PJB/KD)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
